metadata.title:
UNIONS WILL FIGHT THE


WASHINGTON LIQUOR co.

pages.raw_text:
Another union fight is impending
and the elimination will probably be
when the trades council takes up the
question of declaring the Washington
Liquor company unfair. In case such
an order is issued, it will mean that
every union bartender in the city will
be commanded not to handle or sell
goods sold to the retail liquor dealers
through the Washington Liquor COM
pany.


The trouble arises over the old fight
between the unions and the Trow-
bridge company, doing business in ex-
pressage and draying.


S. j. Holland, the principal owner
of the Washington Liquor company,
engaged the Trowbridge people to do
his hauling. At the time the Wash
ington Liquor company had 1T one
union man employed.


The unions served notice on the
Hollands that they must cancel the
contract with the Trowbridge COM
